purpose: Separate externally established cosmological parameters, observational nuisance parameters, standard dark-energy baselines, and Rafael/RLL authorial parameters before any information-criterion or Bayesian comparison.
claim_policy: Parameter origin is provenance metadata only; it does not establish model preference. External parameters may be used as priors, fitted parameters, derived quantities, or nuisance parameters only when the same policy is applied consistently across LCDM, wCDM, CPL and RLL comparisons.
no_plagiarism_policy: This registry stores parameter provenance, roles, equations-by-reference, and citation keys. It must not copy copyrighted prose from source papers. Reports should paraphrase and cite the bibliography keys listed here.

layers.consensus_base: Parameters of the standard cosmological framework or direct observational calibration used by Planck, BAO, SNe, CMB and growth analyses.
layers.standard_extension: Academically established extensions used to test LCDM, such as wCDM, CPL/w0waCDM, curvature, neutrino mass, N_eff and growth amplitude.
layers.observational_nuisance: Calibration or likelihood parameters required by datasets, not physical claims of RLL.
layers.rll_authorial: Rafael/RLL phenomenological parameters. These must be counted in k and must never be presented as external literature constants.

parameters.name: H0
parameters.notes: Present-day expansion rate. Do not hard-clamp at an optimizer boundary without reporting the bound. Run at least: no H0 prior, Planck prior, local-distance-ladder prior, and broad prior.

parameters.name: h
parameters.notes: h = H0/100. Required to interpret physical densities such as omega_b=Omega_b h^2 and omega_c=Omega_c h^2.

parameters.name: omega_b
parameters.notes: Physical baryon density. Needed when r_d or theta_* is derived rather than fixed.

parameters.name: omega_c
parameters.notes: Physical cold dark matter density. Do not replace it silently with Omega_m unless h and baryons are consistently defined.

parameters.name: Om
parameters.notes: Total matter density today, including baryons, cold dark matter and massive-neutrino contribution when present.

parameters.name: Or
parameters.notes: Radiation density should be derived consistently when early-universe or CMB quantities are used.

parameters.name: Ok
parameters.notes: Default fair-comparison route may be flat, but any curved run must release curvature for every compared model and use the curved D_M branch.

parameters.name: rd
parameters.notes: BAO predictions use D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d or D_V/r_d. Within one comparison, r_d must be fixed for all models or derived for all models.

parameters.name: theta_star
parameters.notes: Use with proper covariance or declare a diagonal compressed-likelihood limitation.

parameters.name: R_shift
parameters.notes: CMB shift parameter should not be used as independent diagonal data if an official covariance is available but absent.

parameters.name: w
parameters.notes: Required baseline before attributing improvements to RLL-specific dynamics.

parameters.name: w0
parameters.notes: CPL baseline must be fitted independently. If RLL is projected to an effective w0, label it derived/projection, not fitted RLL evidence.

parameters.name: wa
parameters.notes: Captures time variation of dark-energy equation of state. Do not merge it into RLL parameters unless explicitly running a hybrid model and counting k.

parameters.name: sum_mnu
parameters.notes: Massive neutrinos are degenerate with expansion and growth. Use a shared policy across all models.

parameters.name: Neff
parameters.notes: Needed if early-universe radiation, r_d or CMB-derived constraints are varied beyond the base model.

parameters.name: ln10As
parameters.notes: Required for CMB/growth likelihoods, not needed for a pure background-only distance fit.

parameters.name: ns
parameters.notes: Do not include in k for a background-only run unless actually varied.

parameters.name: tau
parameters.notes: Relevant for full CMB likelihoods; should not be invented for BAO-only or SN-only runs.

parameters.name: sigma8
parameters.notes: Needed for f sigma8 and S8 diagnostics; not interchangeable with S8.

parameters.name: S8
parameters.notes: Usually derived as sigma8 sqrt(Omega_m/0.3). Only count in k if fitted as an independent nuisance/prior parameter.

parameters.name: fsigma8
parameters.notes: If computed by a growth-index proxy, mark proxy mode and block strong growth claims.

parameters.name: M_abs
parameters.notes: SN nuisance/calibration parameter, not an RLL physical parameter. Include covariance policy.

parameters.name: SN_covariance
parameters.notes: Not counted in k, but claim-blocking if missing when Pantheon+ is used.

parameters.name: BAO_covariance
parameters.notes: Not counted in k, but must be applied identically to every model in the same comparison.

parameters.name: Os0
parameters.notes: Authorial RLL amplitude; do not label as external literature parameter.

parameters.name: zt
parameters.notes: Transition redshift; requires prior bounds, sensitivity checks and comparison with CPL effective transition behavior.

parameters.name: wt
parameters.notes: Transition width; must stay positive and be reported as a free parameter.

DESI_DR2_2025.title: DESI DR2 Results II: Measurements of Baryon Acoustic Oscillations and Cosmological Constraints

DESI_Extended_DE_2025.title: DESI DR2 extended dark-energy analysis / w0-wa and dynamical dark-energy context
DESI_Extended_DE_2025.authors: DESI Collaboration and related DR2 dark-energy analyses

PantheonPlus2022.title: The Pantheon+ Analysis: Cosmological Constraints
PantheonPlus2022.authors: Brout, Scolnic, Popovic, Riess et al.

SH0ES2021.title: A Comprehensive Measurement of the Local Value of the Hubble Constant with 1 km/s/Mpc Uncertainty from HST and SH0ES

ChevallierPolarski2001.title: Accelerating Universes with Scaling Dark Matter

Linder2003.title: Exploring the Expansion History of the Universe

CAMB1999.title: Efficient Computation of CMB anisotropies in closed FRW models

CLASS2011.title: The Cosmic Linear Anisotropy Solving System (CLASS) II: Approximation schemes

CosmoMC2002.title: Cosmological parameters from CMB and other data: a Monte-Carlo approach

Cobaya2020.title: Cobaya: Code for Bayesian Analysis of hierarchical physical models

RLL_internal_ansatz.title: RLL logistic transition ansatz
RLL_internal_ansatz.claim_boundary: Internal phenomenological model parameters only; not external literature constants.

integration_rules: Never add a parameter only to improve fit without a literature role and a declared prior policy.
Every fitted physical or nuisance parameter must be counted in k for AIC, AICc and BIC.
Covariance matrices are not free parameters, but missing covariance blocks claim-block the corresponding dataset.
Use the same parameter policy for LCDM, wCDM, CPL and RLL inside a single comparison.
RLL parameters Os0, zt and wt remain authorial phenomenological parameters and must be separated from external cosmological parameters.
A pure background-only run must not claim growth or CMB-spectrum validation.
A growth claim requires CLASS or CAMB benchmark or must be labelled proxy/claim-blocked.
A Pantheon+ claim requires materialized raw files, covariance, hashes and explicit SN nuisance policy.
A DESI BAO claim requires D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d or D_V/r_d predictions using the official covariance policy and dimension/hash checks.

failsafe.rollback: This registry is metadata only; remove or revert this file without touching data/real/** if validation fails.
failsafe.claim_boundary: No superiority claim is allowed unless every fitted/free/nuisance parameter is counted consistently in k and every used dataset has its covariance policy declared.
